item.title: 18th Sunday of Pentecost, The Great Catch Of Fish, The Greatest Miracle Was Not The Fish, 2009-10-11

item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. The Great Catch Of Fish, The Greatest Miracle Was Not The Fish, 2009-10-11

item.title: 18th Sunday of Pentecost, The First Great Catch Of Fish, We are not saved by works of the law, but by being with Christ. The second great catch of fish illustrates this., 2010-10-10
item.description: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. The first Great catch of fish, read in context with the 2nd great catch (which occurred three years later, 8 days after the resurrection) is truly the "Gospel" - the "Good News", because it is a promise to us that we can change and truly become holy - all we need do is be with Jesus Christ throughout our life. We compare the two great catches, and explore what it means to be with Christ. The Epistle reading is from Galatians, them overall theme of which is that we cannot be saved bu . We are not saved by works, but we cannot be saved unless we stay with Christ throughout our life - we examine what this means.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. The first Great catch of fish, read in context with the 2nd great catch (which occurred three years later, 8 days after the resurrection) is truly the "Gospel" - the "Good News", because it is a promise to us that we can change and truly become holy - all we need do is be with Jesus Christ throughout our life. We compare the two great catches, and explore what it means to be with Christ. The Epistle reading is from Galatians, them overall theme of which is that we cannot be saved bu . We are not saved by works, but we cannot be saved unless we stay with Christ throughout our life - we examine what this means.

item.title: 18th Sunday of Pentecost, 2012-10-07
item.description: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. When Jesus commanded Peter to &quotLaunch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught;", He was giving a principle, by which, and only by which we will stand in the presence of God, even in this life. This principle is all over the Scriptures. It involves patience, courage, obedience and INTENSITY. Although prayer is not explicitly mentioned, it is also about prayer, and especially about the Jesus prayer. How and why must we "launch out into the deep"?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. When Jesus commanded Peter to &quotLaunch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught;", He was giving a principle, by which, and only by which we will stand in the presence of God, even in this life. This principle is all over the Scriptures. It involves patience, courage, obedience and INTENSITY. Although prayer is not explicitly mentioned, it is also about prayer, and especially about the Jesus prayer. How and why must we "launch out into the deep"?

item.title: 18th Sunday of Pentecost, 2014-10-12
item.description: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. The Epistle and Gospel today (2 Corinthians 9:6-11 Luke 6:31-36) contain two internal laws that we must follow if we are to become completely happy. We discuss these related laws and the good news that is contained in them! We must learn to read the scripture with skill and understand its internal laws
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :18th Sunday of Pentecost. The Epistle and Gospel today (2 Corinthians 9:6-11 Luke 6:31-36) contain two internal laws that we must follow if we are to become completely happy. We discuss these related laws and the good news that is contained in them! We must learn to read the scripture with skill and understand its internal laws
